metadata.title: Global Crypto Mining Regulations & Hashrate Distribution
metadata.notes: Hashrate percentages from Q1 2026. Tax and regulatory data as of early 2026. Some countries have unofficial/underground mining not captured in official hashrate figures.

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal, state-level variation
key_policies: No federal mining ban; regulated at state level
Texas and Wyoming most mining-friendly states
Form 1099-DA required from exchanges/brokers starting 2025
Trump administration generally pro-crypto since 2025

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as ordinary income at fair market value when received
tax_treatment.deductions: Hardware depreciation, electricity costs deductible as business expenses

energy_environment_policies: No federal energy restrictions on mining
Some states (e.g., New York) restrict proof-of-work mining using fossil fuels
Texas offers demand-response incentives for miners to curtail during grid stress
Diverse energy mix: renewables, natural gas, nuclear
countries.recent_changes: Pro-crypto executive orders under Trump administration; FIT21 framework advancing in Congress

countries.regulatory_stance: Mining legal, crypto payments banned domestically
key_policies: Mining legalized under 2024 Digital Currency Mining Law
Licensing required for large-scale operations
Using crypto for domestic payments is prohibited
Foreign participation limited by sanctions and geopolitical risk
Regional mining bans in some energy-stressed areas (e.g., Dagestan, Chechnya)

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as business income under standard corporate/income tax
tax_treatment.deductions: Business expense deductions available for registered miners

energy_environment_policies: Low-cost natural gas and hydropower in Siberia
Cold climate reduces cooling costs
Some regions ban mining due to grid strain (Irkutsk region imposed winter restrictions)
Government balancing energy export revenue vs. mining consumption
countries.recent_changes: Comprehensive mining regulation law took effect 2024-2025; regional bans in energy-deficit areas expanded in 2025

countries.regulatory_stance: Complete ban on mining and crypto transactions (since 2021)
key_policies: Total ban on crypto mining enacted September 2021
All crypto transactions prohibited
Renewed crackdowns in Xinjiang province December 2025
Authorities actively monitor electricity consumption for illegal mining
Despite ban, significant underground mining persists

tax_treatment.mining_income: N/A (activity is illegal)

energy_environment_policies: Ban partly motivated by energy consumption and carbon neutrality goals
Electricity monitoring used to detect illegal mining
Underground operations often use seasonal hydropower in Sichuan/Yunnan
countries.recent_changes: December 2025 renewed crackdown in Xinjiang; underground mining still estimated at ~12% of global hashrate

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal with new registration requirements
key_policies: Mining is legal with regulatory framework encouraging development
Mandatory registration for miners approved December 2024
DNIT requires reporting of digital asset transactions over $5,000
Regulatory inconsistency remains a concern

tax_treatment.mining_income: Subject to standard business taxation; comprehensive tax framework in development
tax_treatment.deductions: Business expense deductions for registered operations

energy_environment_policies: Surplus hydroelectric capacity from Itaipu Dam (world's second largest)
Among the lowest electricity costs globally
Energy abundance is the primary driver of mining growth
countries.recent_changes: Mandatory miner registration and transaction reporting adopted late 2024; oversight implementation continuing through 2026

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal and government-supported
key_policies: Dubai's Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) provides comprehensive licensing
Abu Dhabi Global Market has separate regulatory framework
Government actively promotes crypto/blockchain sector
Coordinated strategy with state-level involvement in mining

tax_treatment.mining_income: 0% personal income tax

energy_environment_policies: Diversified energy sources (solar, natural gas, nuclear)
Extreme heat requires advanced immersion cooling solutions
Government investing in sustainable energy for mining
countries.recent_changes: Continued expansion of VARA licensing framework; growing institutional mining operations

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal with provincial variation
key_policies: Clear federal regulations for crypto
Must register as money service business if exchanging crypto
Quebec allocates specific energy blocks to mining operations
Policy fragmentation across provinces creates uneven growth

tax_treatment.mining_income: Hobby mining: not taxed on receipt, capital gains on disposal (50% inclusion rate). Business mining: income tax on receipt
tax_treatment.deductions: Business miners can deduct electricity, hardware, and operational costs

energy_environment_policies: Abundant hydroelectric resources in Quebec and British Columbia
Quebec paused new mining connections in 2018, later created dedicated allocation
Alberta has cheaper natural gas-based electricity
Cold climate reduces cooling costs
countries.recent_changes: Provincial energy policies continuing to evolve; some provinces tightening allocations

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal, government-supported for energy monetization
key_policies: Government views mining as way to monetize underutilized hydroelectric capacity
One of the fastest-growing mining markets globally
Grew from 0% hashrate in 2021 to ~2.6% in Q1 2026
Political instability poses long-term operational risk

tax_treatment.mining_income: Standard business taxation; specific crypto tax framework still developing

energy_environment_policies: Massive underutilized hydroelectric capacity (Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam)
Government eager to monetize excess energy via compute/mining
Among cheapest electricity globally for mining operations
countries.recent_changes: Rapid growth trajectory; hashrate share nearly doubled from 1.5% (Q1 2025) to 2.6% (Q1 2026)

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal with licensing and energy restrictions
key_policies: Licensing regime required for all mining operations
Elevated electricity rates for miners
Subject to energy capacity restrictions and grid caps
Declining from post-China-ban highs due to regulation

tax_treatment.mining_income: Commercial operation taxation; additional electricity surcharge acts as de facto mining tax

energy_environment_policies: Grid strain from mining growth post-2021 China ban
Government imposed energy caps and elevated rates for miners
Coal-based energy raises environmental concerns
Infrastructure limitations constrain further growth
countries.recent_changes: Continued decline in hashrate share as regulation tightens and energy costs rise

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal under EU MiCA framework
key_policies: Subject to EU MiCA regulation (Markets in Crypto-Assets)
BaFin oversees crypto activities
DAC8 (Crypto Asset Tax Transparency Act / KStTG) effective January 2026
Crypto service providers must auto-report all user data to tax authorities

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as business or miscellaneous income
tax_treatment.deductions: Annual exemption limit of 1,000 EUR; business expenses deductible

energy_environment_policies: High electricity costs make mining largely uncompetitive
EU environmental sustainability requirements for crypto operations
Post-nuclear phase-out, energy costs remain among highest in Europe
countries.recent_changes: DAC8 implementation from January 2026 requiring automated reporting of all crypto transactions to German tax authorities

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal, regulated by Securities Commission
key_policies: Crypto mining is legal but taxable as business activity
Securities Commission Malaysia regulates digital asset exchanges
No specific crypto mining laws; falls under general income tax
Illegal electricity theft for mining is heavily prosecuted

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as income if conducted as business for profit
tax_treatment.capital_gains_long: Capital gains tax introduced 2024 on disposals

energy_environment_policies: Subsidized electricity has attracted illegal mining operations
Government cracking down on electricity theft by miners
Tropical climate increases cooling costs
countries.recent_changes: Ongoing crackdowns on illegal mining operations stealing electricity; capital gains tax on crypto disposals introduced

countries.regulatory_stance: Mining legal with license; crypto payments banned domestically
key_policies: Mining legalized in 2019 with licensing requirement
Licensed miners must sell mined BTC to central bank to fund imports
Central Bank of Iran (CBI) is sole regulatory authority
Using digital currency for domestic payments strictly prohibited
Mining operations halted seasonally during peak summer electricity demand

tax_treatment.mining_income: Licensed miners must sell to central bank; taxed as business income

energy_environment_policies: Licensed miners receive subsidized electricity in exchange for selling BTC to central bank
Ministry of Energy imposes consumption ceilings on licensed miners
Mining consumes ~2% of national electricity production
Operations halted during summer peak demand periods
Widespread illegal mining exacerbates grid strain
countries.recent_changes: January 2025 directive formalizing crypto market regulations; $7.78 billion crypto ecosystem in 2025; central bank accumulated $507M in USDT

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal with energy allocation caps
key_policies: No ban on crypto mining
Over 10 mining facilities operating
Sector expected to grow 20% in 2026
Power companies cap new capacity at 25 MW per site

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as business income

energy_environment_policies: 100% renewable energy (geothermal and hydroelectric)
Low electricity prices
Cold climate dramatically reduces cooling costs
Power companies trimming new capacity allocations (25 MW cap per site)
Investment in immersion cooling to reduce electricity draw
countries.recent_changes: Power companies implementing per-site capacity caps; immersion cooling adoption growing

countries.regulatory_stance: Existing operations legal; new mining data centers banned since October 2025
key_policies: Temporary ban on NEW crypto mining data centers (October 2025)
Existing mining operations allowed to continue
Mandatory national data center registry (first in Europe) since January 2025
All data centers must register with Norwegian Communications Authority (Nkom)
Fines up to 5% of annual turnover for non-compliance

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as business income

energy_environment_policies: Government prefers allocating renewable power to manufacturing, heating, or AI workloads over mining
Nearly 100% renewable electricity (hydroelectric)
New mining facilities blocked to preserve power for other uses
Cold climate favorable for cooling
countries.recent_changes: October 2025: temporary ban on new crypto mining data centers; mandatory data center registry launched January 2025

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal but increasingly hostile regulatory environment
key_policies: Crypto-Asset Environmental Transparency Act effective January 2025
Mining operations >0.5 MW must publish real-time energy consumption data
Banks cutting off services to mining companies
68% of Swedish mining companies planned to relocate by 2026

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as business or hobby income

energy_environment_policies: Crypto-Asset Environmental Transparency Act (Jan 2025) mandates energy reporting
Swedish Energy Agency investing 150M SEK ($13.8M) in waste heat recovery projects
Pilot in Lulea recovering 65% of mining heat for district heating
Banks de-banking mining companies despite renewable energy use
Strong push to redirect energy from mining to other uses
countries.recent_changes: Environmental Transparency Act (Jan 2025); mass exodus of miners planned; banking access increasingly difficult

countries.regulatory_stance: Fully legal and government-championed
key_policies: Bitcoin adopted as legal tender in 2021
Government actively mines Bitcoin using volcanic geothermal energy
Full tax exemption for foreign crypto investors
Bitcoin bonds (Volcano Bonds) launched

tax_treatment.mining_income: 0% for foreign investors

energy_environment_policies: Government-backed volcanic geothermal mining operation
Renewable energy focus for mining
Small-scale compared to major mining countries
countries.recent_changes: Continued government commitment to Bitcoin despite IMF pressure; Volcano Bond issuance

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal; comprehensive regulatory framework being built (2025-2027)
key_policies: Individual mining is legal without a license
Commercial mining services require FCA compliance
New FSMA-based crypto regime: applications open Sept 2026, go-live Oct 2027
CARF-aligned reporting obligations from January 2026

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as miscellaneous income or trading income depending on scale
tax_treatment.deductions: Annual CGT allowance of 3,000 GBP; business expenses deductible

energy_environment_policies: High electricity costs make mining largely uncompetitive
Government discourages high energy use but no mining-specific energy restrictions
No dedicated energy allocation for mining
countries.recent_changes: CARF reporting from Jan 2026; FCA authorization gateway opening Sept 2026; new comprehensive regime by Oct 2027

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal with new comprehensive framework (2026)
key_policies: Mining legal, not regulated by specific crypto laws
Virtual Assets Law (2022) operationalized via BCB resolutions (Nov 2025)
Compliance deadline February 2026 with 9-month grace period
Miners must comply with standard tax registration and reporting

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as income
tax_treatment.deductions: Sales under R$35,000/month exempt; CAMEX reduced import tax on SHA-256 mining equipment to 0% (through Dec 2025)

energy_environment_policies: Large hydroelectric capacity
No specific energy restrictions on mining
Environmental concerns about mining in Amazon/wetland regions
countries.recent_changes: BCB published operational crypto resolutions Nov 2025; compliance deadline Feb 2026; mining equipment import tariff reduction expired Dec 2025

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal with VASP registration framework
key_policies: Law 27,739 (March 2024) created VASP framework under CNV oversight
CNV Resolution 1058/2025 establishes mandatory VASP registration
Businesses with monthly volumes >35,000 UVA (~$29,246) must register
Full enforcement from December 31, 2025

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as income; crypto treated as intangible property
tax_treatment.capital_gains_long: Holding crypto is not taxed; profits from transactions are taxable
tax_treatment.deductions: Tax amnesty program (Blanqueo) offered 5-15% rates; first $100K exempt if kept in Argentine bank

energy_environment_policies: Industrial electricity ~$0.03-0.04/kWh (competitive)
Patagonia offers cheap energy and cold climate
No specific mining energy restrictions
countries.recent_changes: VASP registration mandatory from late 2025; CARF data collection begins 2026 for international reporting by 2027-2028

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal, unregulated activity; favorable tax changes
key_policies: Mining is legal and not subject to specific regulation
Revenue Department treats mining as manufacturing (taxable under Section 40(8))
5-year personal income exemption on crypto capital gains (2025-2029) via licensed exchanges
Mining income does NOT qualify for the tax exemption (business income)

tax_treatment.mining_income: Taxed as business income (does not qualify for 2025-2029 exemption)
tax_treatment.capital_gains_short: 0% via licensed exchange (2025-2029 exemption); otherwise up to 35%
tax_treatment.capital_gains_long: 0% via licensed exchange (2025-2029 exemption); otherwise up to 35%

energy_environment_policies: No specific energy restrictions on mining
Moderate electricity costs
Tropical climate increases cooling costs
countries.recent_changes: 5-year crypto capital gains tax exemption launched January 2025 (for licensed exchange transactions only)

countries.regulatory_stance: Ambiguous; no clear mining regulations but heavy taxation
key_policies: No clear regulations specifically for mining
30% flat tax on all crypto gains (no loss offset between tokens)
1% TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) on all transactions
Some states restrict mining due to energy concerns
RBI historically hostile to crypto but no outright ban

tax_treatment.mining_income: 30% flat tax on gains
tax_treatment.deductions: Losses CANNOT offset gains from different tokens; very restrictive

energy_environment_policies: Some states restrict mining operations due to energy concerns
High and variable electricity costs across states
No dedicated energy policy for mining
countries.recent_changes: 30% tax and 1% TDS remain in effect; no softening of stance expected

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal and crypto-friendly; tax-free for individuals
key_policies: One of the most crypto-friendly jurisdictions globally
0% tax on crypto for individuals
Crypto not considered Georgian-sourced income
Historical hub for mining due to cheap electricity

tax_treatment.mining_income: 0% for individuals; 15% corporate tax for businesses
tax_treatment.deductions: N/A for individuals (no tax); corporate deductions available

energy_environment_policies: Cheap hydroelectric power
Cold mountainous climate reduces cooling costs
No energy restrictions on mining
countries.recent_changes: Remains one of the most favorable jurisdictions for individual crypto miners

countries.regulatory_stance: Legal only within Gelephu Mindfulness City; state-backed mining
key_policies: Mining and exchanges permitted only for Gelephu Mindfulness City (GMC) registered entities
Royal Monetary Authority (RMA) regulates crypto activities
Government holds ~11,711 BTC (~$1.3 billion as of mid-2025)
State-backed mining operation using hydroelectric power

tax_treatment.mining_income: Governed by GMC-specific regulations

energy_environment_policies: Abundant hydroelectric power (Bhutan exports electricity to India)
State-run mining leverages surplus hydroelectric capacity
One of the largest government Bitcoin holdings globally relative to GDP
countries.recent_changes: April 2025 RMA notice restricting crypto mining/exchanges to GMC-registered entities only

status_legend.legal: Mining is legal with standard business regulations
status_legend.legal_restricted: Mining is legal but with significant restrictions (licensing, energy caps, geographic limits, or payment bans)
status_legend.banned: Mining is officially prohibited (may still occur underground)
status_legend.ambiguous: No clear legal framework; regulatory status uncertain
